content.full_text:
October 3, 1989


Steven R. Crooks
Hefner, Stark & Marois
2710 Gateway Oaks Dr., Ste. 300 South
Sacramento, CA  95833-3501

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-89-464

Dear Mr. Crooks:

You have requested advice on behalf of the political action
committee of the Building Industry Association, BAC-PAC,
concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act.¹

QUESTIONS

1.  When disclosing member contributions received through
the collection of association dues, has BAC-PAC received the
contributions when the members' dues are received by the Building
Industry Association (the "Association") or when the Association
transmits the funds to BAC-PAC?

2.  If the date of receipt by BAC-PAC is the date the
Association receives the members' dues, what are the Association's
recordkeeping responsibilities with regard to such contributions?

3.  Is there a requirement that the Association transmit
member contributions at any particular times during the year?

CONCLUSIONS

1.  BAC-PAC receives the members' contributions when the
dues payments are received by the Association.

2.  The Association must report the contributions to BAC-PAC
no later than the closing date of the campaign statement on which
the contributions must be disclosed by the committee.  Therefore,

_________________________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 81000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

Steven R. Crooks
Page 2


the Association must keep whatever records are reasonably
necessary for BAC-PAC to comply with its disclosure obligations.

     3.  The Act does not specify when the Association must
transmit member contributions to BAC-PAC.

                                FACTS

     BAC-PAC is the sponsored committee of the Building Industry
Association.  Contributions to BAC-PAC are collected as a portion
of dues paid to the Association by its members.  During our
telephone conversation of September 8, 1989, you indicated that
the members have earmarked a specified portion of their dues to be
contributed to the committee.

                                ANALYSIS

     The Act requires committees established pursuant to Section
82013(a) ("recipient committees") to file periodic reports
disclosing, among other things, contributions received.  (Sections
82000-84221.)  The recordkeeping section of the 1989 Information
Manual on Campaign Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform
Act states:

     A contribution is "received" the day the candidate
     or committee treasurer or his or her agent actually
     receives the contribution, not when the contribution
     is deposited into the committee's bank account.

                              Information Manual, p. 52.
                              (Emphasis added.)

     Because the Association's members have earmarked a specified
portion of their dues as contributions to BAC-PAC, the committee
"receives" the contributions on the date the Association receives
the dues from the members.

     With regard to recordkeeping responsibilities, Section 84306
provides:

     ...All contributions received by a person acting as
     an agent of a committee shall be reported promptly to
     the committee's treasurer or any of his or her desig-
     nated agents.  "Promptly" as used in this section
     means not later than the closing date of any campaign
     statement the committee or candidate for whom the
     contribution is intended is required to file.

     As the agent of the committee for the receipt of contributions,
the Association must report to BAC-PAC the contributions it
collects.  In order to provide the committee with adequate
information to fulfill its filing obligations, the Association
should record and provide to the committee the name, address,

Steven R. Crooks
Page 3


occupation and employer of each person who has earmarked $25 or
more in contributions during the calendar year.

     Finally, as noted in the conclusions stated above, the Act
does not specify when contributions collected by an agent of a
committee must actually be transmitted to the committee.

     Please call me at (916) 322-5662 if you have additional
questions.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Kathryn E. Dovovan
                                        General Counsel

                                        Carla D. Wardlow
                                        By: Carla S. Wardlow
                                            Assistant Chief, Technical
                                            Assistance & Analysis Division

HEPNER
STARK &
MAROIS
Law Offices

FPPC

AUG 09 1989

RE:

2710 Gateway Oaks Drive
Suite 300 South
Sacramento, CA 95811-3501
(916) 925-6620
Fax # 925-1127

Bay Area Office
(415) 837-2131

August 7, 1989

Jeanne Pritchard
Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Assistance Division
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Re: BAC-PAC, Sponsored Committee of the Building
Industry Association

Dear Ms. Pritchard:

This letter is a request for formal written advice
from the Commission pursuant to Government Code § 83114(b)
and Commission Regulation 18329. I am writing as the
authorized representative for the political action
committee of the Building Industry Association, BAC-PAC.
BAC-PAC is a sponsored recipient committee of the BIA,
which receives most of its contributions from a percentage
of members' dues received by BIA. BIA then makes transfers
of that percentage of members' dues which are earmarked as
contributions, to the PAC on a periodic basis.

My question, on behalf of BAC-PAC and BIA, is whether
the member contributions to BAC-PAC for members' dues are
deemed received by BAC-PAC when the members' dues are
received by BIA, or when BIA transfers the actual
contribution amounts to BAC-PAC? Also, if the
contributions are deemed received by BAC-PAC when the
members' dues are received by BIA, what recordkeeping
responsibilities does BIA have under the Political Reform
Act concerning such contributions, for example, under
Government Code § 84104 and Commission Regulation 18401.
Finally, as a general question, do any provisions of the
Political Reform Act or any of the Commission regulations
(especially the new statutes and regulations promulgated
under Proposition 73), have any impact on the timing of
when BIA physically transfers the members' contributions to
BAC-PAC, that is, is it preferable or required that such
transfers be made at any particular times during a calendar
or fiscal year?

[Left side list of names - office counsel:]
Archur Hefner
(1917-1984)
Theodore M. Marois, Jr.
James M. Woodside
Curtis A. Hepner
Kenneth R. Stone
Timothy D. Herdt
Ruth Campos Abelson

Barbara E. Herndon

Rosalind M. Sargis
Susan L. Morrow

Joseph E. Limon
Peggy J Herner
James J. Davidson
Teresa M. Meegan
Steven R. Crooks
Leo W. Burns
Delbert W. Orris
Ralph J Ferguson
Margaret Donahue
Lisa A. White-Wright
Jeffrey H. Grayell
Bruce A. Morey
Daniel W. Smith
Douglas R. Horne
Kirk F. Giberson
Michelle J. Cook
Diane R. Sherbondy
Jody J. Burchers
Maria J. Womerslager
Marshall B. Jagush
Julie B. Gustavson
Scott B. Harwood

Of Counsel
Robert N. Stark
Robert W. Bell

HEFNER
STARK &
MAROIS
Law Offices

Jeanne Pritchard
August 7, 1989
Page 2

Thank you for your assistance with this advice
request. Please do not hesitate to contact me if you need
additional information to consider these issues.

very truly yours,

HEFNER, STARK & MAROIS

By [illegible]
Steven R. Crooks

SRC:11

cc: Dwight Hansen

047-9109

AUG 0 9 1989

2710 Gateway Oaks Drive
Suite 300 South
Sacramento, CA 95833-3501
(916) 925-6620

(415) 837-2131

HEFNER
STARK &
MAROIS
Law Offices

August 7, 1989

Jeanne Pritchard
Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Assistance Division
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Re: BAC-PAC, Sponsored Committee of the Building
Industry Association

Dear Ms. Pritchard:

This letter is a request for formal written advice
from the Commission pursuant to Government Code § 83114(b)
and Commission Regulation 18329. I am writing as the
authorized representative for the political action
committee of the Building Industry Association, BAC-PAC.
BAC-PAC is a sponsored recipient committee of the BIA,
which receives most of its contributions from a percentage
of members' dues received by BIA. BIA then makes transfers
of that percentage of members' dues which are earmarked as
contributions, to the PAC on a periodic basis.

My question, on behalf of BAC-PAC and BIA, is whether
the member contributions to BAC-PAC for members' dues are
deemed received by BAC-PAC when the members' dues are
received by BIA or when BIA transfers the actual
contribution amounts to BAC-PAC? Also, if the
contributions are deemed received by BAC-PAC when the
members' dues are received by BIA, what recordkeeping
responsibilities does BIA have under the Political Reform
Act concerning such contributions, for example, under
Government Code § 84104 and Commission Regulation 18401.
Finally, as a general question, do any provisions of the
Political Reform Act or any of the Commission regulations
(especially the new statutes and regulations promulgated
under Proposition 73), have any impact on the timing of
when BIA physically transfers the members' contributions to
BAC-PAC, that is, is it preferable or required that such
transfers be made at any particular times during a calendar
or fiscal year?

Archie Hefner
(1919-1982)
Theodore M. Marois, Jr.
Marshall M. Woodside
John D. Bessey
Kenneth Levy

Robert N. Stark

Jeanne Pritchard
August 7, 1989
Page 2

Thank you for your assistance with this advice
request. Please do not hesitate to contact me if you need
additional information to consider these issues.

Very truly yours,

HEFNER, STARK & MAROIS

By [illegible]
Steven R. Crooks

SRC:11

cc: Dwight Hansen

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

August 10, 1989


Steven R. Crooks
Hefner, Stark & Marois
2710 Gateway Oaks Dr., Ste. 300 So.
Sacramento, CA 95833-3501

Re:  Letter No. 49-464

Dear Mr. Crooks:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on August 9, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission.  If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice.  If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the
information needed.  If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can.  (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

     You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,


Jeanne Pritchard
Chief Technical Assistance
and Analysis Division
